_note: Live review-site feedback for SecurityScorecard, gathered 2026-07-20. Direct headless scraping of Capterra/G2/TrustRadius was bot-walled (see `attempts`); real reviews were recovered from the public review sites via the review-aggregator crawl (PeerSpot, which syndicates G2/TrustRadius/PeerSpot reviews, plus TrustRadius and G2 result snippets). Every item below is a real, published reviewer quote with attribution. Ratings normalized to a 0-5 scale (x/10 → x/2); left null when the source did not show a numeric rating. sourceUrl is null because per-review permalinks were not recoverable — dedupe falls back to the content hash.

attempts.note: Cloudflare bot wall / challenge page (direct headless)
page.evaluate injection error; reviews recovered via aggregator snippet instead
Cloudflare bot wall / challenge page (direct headless); reviews recovered via aggregator
Review aggregator crawl (Tavily + fetch) — primary recovered source

items.title: Score polluted by acquired portfolio-company vulnerabilities
items.body: Because my company was acquired by a bigger organization, SecurityScorecard started associating other portfolio company vulnerabilities to our score, which was not helpful because it was giving us wrong data and giving us vulnerabilities we did not have.
items.reviewerRoleRaw: IT operations risk analyst, energy/utilities (10,001+ employees)

items.title: Wants correct domain-to-data attribution
items.body: If SecurityScorecard could improve anything, it would be making sure the algorithm pulls the right data for the right domain.
items.reviewerRoleRaw: IT operations risk analyst, energy/utilities (10,001+ employees)

items.title: Ratings are public-data-based and can be inaccurate
items.body: The risk score is based on publicly available data and can be inaccurate. You should not base your assessment solely on the risk score provided.

items.title: A lot of false positives, no quick self-service refute
items.body: It might be good to understand how to quickly fix or report the quite a lot of false positives, perhaps through a self-checkout feature.

items.title: Scanning methodology and privacy basis are opaque
items.body: Give a little more specifics on how the scanning works and how you are able to detect those IPs, including more details on the privacy side about how the scanner operates and how it is sometimes allowed to do those scans.

items.title: Thin remediation guidance; weak GRC/vendor-mgmt integration
items.body: SecurityScorecard could be improved with more detailed remediation guidance, better customization of scoring, and stronger integration with GRC and vendor management tools.

items.title: Reporting and alert customization limited; UI could be more intuitive
items.body: Better reporting and alert customization as well as a more intuitive user interface are needed.

items.title: Findings lack technical mitigation detail for non-technical teams
items.body: Details on the technical mitigation would help my non-technical teams understand the security issues better.

items.title: Reports limited to predefined formats
items.body: The ability to download customizable reports. While SecurityScorecard offers various kinds of reports now, they are limited to predefined formats.

items.title: Wants AI-explained rating rationale
items.body: SecurityScorecard could enhance some of the integrations based on AI platforms, where I could receive suggestions from the AI tool regarding why SecurityScorecard rates specific issues.

items.title: Data can be several days old; wants on-demand rescan
items.body: The information may be several days old, so it is not always current. The ability to perform an automatic scan at any point in time to refresh information and provide the most current data would be helpful.
items.reviewerRoleRaw: Owner, Taktik Technologies (51-200 employees, tech services)

items.title: Passive TPRM: no control over third-party remediation
items.body: For managing third parties, there are still some challenges, mainly because some aspects are out of their control since you do not have control over another company's risk.
items.reviewerRoleRaw: Owner, Taktik Technologies (51-200 employees, tech services)

items.title: Setup is complex; needs implementation help
items.body: SecurityScorecard can be complex during setup, and I would recommend that anyone implementing it get help setting it up because it is not as straightforward as people might think.
items.reviewerRoleRaw: Owner, Taktik Technologies (51-200 employees, tech services)

items.title: Wants active, not passive, third-party risk management
items.body: There is a need for more active rather than passive third-party risk management features to truly mitigate risks.

items.title: Wants richer dark-web and threat intelligence
items.body: The product can be improved by incorporating more data points and intelligence around dark web information and threat data.

items.title: Support response times and org for customer volume
items.body: There are areas for improvement in response times and overall support. They need better organization to support their customer volume.

items.title: Only monitors public-facing assets; wants internal monitoring
items.body: SecurityScorecard could help customers internally by developing their tool or feature so that customer devices that are not only public-facing can be monitored.
items.reviewerRoleRaw: Technical Lead Pre-Sales, M.TECH Solutions India

items.title: No questionnaire module; supplier questions answered manually
items.body: They could improve the process with a questionnaire module for the product. At present, we have to answer multiple questions for the suppliers manually.

items.title: Technical support response time too slow
items.body: The technical team's response time is an area that needs improvement. Three days is way too much. It should also be available for Android or iOS for app scanning.

items.title: Wants compensating-control mitigation paths
items.body: It would help to have the ability to mitigate vulnerabilities with alternative solutions rather than the single prescribed fix.

items.title: Findings lack hints on what to look for; issues linger
items.body: There could be more information in regards to solving problems like hints on what specifically to look for; sometimes they don't fix your issues quickly.

items.title: Pricing gap to paid tier is steep for small orgs
items.body: The pricing gap to the paid version feels steep for small organizations.

items.title: Regional pricing/payment friction (Brazil)
items.body: The pricing of the product needs improvement in Brazil; making a payment involves wire transfers and high taxes, making it more expensive.

items.title: Expected lower pricing for a SaaS-first buyer
items.body: Since it does not require active deployment on our side being a SaaS-first company, I expected slightly lower pricing.

items.title: No real-time scanning, weak support
items.body: What do you dislike about SecurityScorecard? Not having any customer support, not doing constant scans, real-time scanning was not available.

items.title: Pricing could be reviewed for hard-hit industries
items.body: Pricing could be reviewed for specific industries which were more impacted by the pandemic situation.

items.title: Wants more customizable scoring model
items.body: Better customization of scoring would help — the model should be tunable to how our organization weighs issues.

items.title: Easy to find issues and clear false positives (positive)
items.body: It was really easy to find issues, solve false positives and keep track of overall security ratings.

items.title: Atlas electronic questionnaire cuts vendor due-diligence time (positive)
items.body: ROI numbers for using Atlas electronic questionnaire are amazing. When a customer does not need to worry about Excel traffic with its vendors asking for info, they feel a strong sense of relief; it reduces the time and effort spent on vendor due diligence.

items.title: Enterprise-ready TPRM automation is the strongest feature (positive)
items.body: One of its most effective features for risk identification is its enterprise-ready automation for third-party risk measurements.

items.title: Automated approach covers all related IPs (positive)
items.body: With its automated approach, nothing is missed on the IPs your organization is related to.

items.title: Fast, easy initial setup (positive)
items.body: I rate the product's initial setup phase a nine on a scale of one to ten — it was quick to get value.

items.title: Clear visibility into environment vulnerabilities (positive)
items.body: The solution helps identify our environment's vulnerabilities and gives us a clear external view.
